extraction.one_line_summary: PETERSON Mads won a reduced bunch sprint after CLARK Simon and DEMARCHI Alessandro's breakaway was caught inside 200 meters following hesitation.
tactical_patterns: CLARK and DEMARCHI hesitated in final 800m with 33-second gap, allowing catch inside 200m—experience gap fatal.
THOMAS Geraint and SCHRODLICH suffered mechanicals <15km remaining, forcing team TTT through convoy on flat finale stage.
CAVENDISH Mark dropped on first categorized climb, confirming knee injury from previous crash limits climbing tolerance significantly.
Peloton failed to organize chase until <3km despite break having only 1:03 at 15km—teams unwilling to commit.

rider_signals.PETERSON Mads: positive: completed grand tour stage win grand slam, outsprinted MILAN in technical finale positioning
rider_signals.CLARK Simon: negative: tactical inexperience evident—sat up with 33s gap at 3km, allowed catch from winning position
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: neutral: second place maintains points jersey but couldn't outsprint PETERSON despite leadout advantage
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: dropped on Category 3 climb confirms knee injury severely limits even moderate climbing
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: late mechanical required team TTT through convoy, exposes GC vulnerability on seemingly safe stages
